pattern: Maximize by replacing the first non-9 digit with 9; minimize by replacing the leading digit with 1 (or any non-leading non-zero digit with 0), respecting the no-leading-zero constraint.
hints: To get the maximum value, find the leftmost digit that is not already 9 and replace every occurrence of it with 9.
To get the minimum value, if the leading digit is not 1, replace all occurrences of it with 1; otherwise find the leftmost digit after the first that is not 0 or 1 and replace it with 0.
The answer is max minus min; both transformations operate on the same original number independently.

edge_inputs.description: all same digit, maximizing gives all 9s, minimizing gives all 1s
single digit 9 — max stays 9, min becomes 1
single digit 1 — max becomes 9, min stays 1
num=10, first digit 1 goes to 1 (no change), second digit 0 already 0
leading digit already 1; min must change another digit to 0
number has digits already at 9; only the non-9 gets changed
all digits 1 — min stays same, max changes to all 9s

stress.description: random integers across the full constraint range
